info.name: Profile – Email Change (POST /email/request-change + POST /email/verify-current + POST /email/confirm-change)
info.description:
E2E tests for the three-step email change flow.

Folder execution order (single invocation via make e2e-email):
  1. setup              — register gmail_test_email, fetch OTP, verify email, login → echg_access_token
  2. happy-path         — step1 (request-change) → fetch step1 OTP → step2 (verify-current) → fetch step2 OTP → step3 (confirm-change) → re-login → GET /me confirm DB write
  3. failures           — cooldown_active, same_email, email_taken, wrong-code, invalid_grant_token
  4. auth-failures      — missing Authorization → 401 on all 3 endpoints
  5. validation         — body > 1 MiB → 413; malformed JSON → 400
  6. rate-limiting-req  — echg:usr: burst=3; dedicated +echgrlreq alias user
  7. rate-limiting-vfy  — echg:usr:vfy: burst=5; dedicated +echgrlvfy alias user + one step1 seed
  8. rate-limiting-cnf  — echg:usr:cnf: burst=5; dedicated +echgrlcnf alias user, bad grant_token warmup

All rate-limiters are user-keyed (JWT available at every endpoint). Running all folders in a single
Newman invocation preserves collection variables (echg_access_token, etc.) across folders.
Redis is flushed once before the run (_e2e-clean); no mid-run flush is needed.

Variable prefix: echg_

Notes on what is NOT covered here and why:
  - T-02–T-04: individual new_email validation variants → handler_test.go
  - T-03: code format variants → handler_test.go
  - T-08: ErrUserNotFound → 500 → handler_test.go
  - T-09, T-20, T-21, T-38, T-39: context.WithoutCancel → service_test.go
  - T-10: store error prefix wrapping → service_test.go
  - T-11, T-12: DB-level token state → store_test.go
  - T-19, T-33: wrong-code increment call count → service_test.go
  - T-29: grant_token user_id mismatch → service_test.go
  - T-34: ErrEmailTaken inside tx (race) → store_test.go T-41
  - T-36, T-37: RevokeAllRefreshTokens + Blocklist called → confirmed indirectly by re-login test
  - T-41: SetUserEmailZero → ErrUserNotFound → store_test.go

All test users use gmail_test_email or @e2e.test addresses so `make _e2e-db-clean` can wipe them.

exec: // Increment the request sequence counter and derive a unique X-Forwarded-For IP
// for every request so non-RL folders never exhaust any rate-limit bucket.

item.name: setup
item.description: Registers the gmail_test_email user, fetches the verification OTP from Gmail, verifies the email, and logs in. Stores echg_access_token for use by subsequent folders.

exec: console.error('[ERROR] gmail_test_email not set in environment — add it to environment.json.');
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for verification email to arrive in Gmail...');
console.log('[INFO] Done waiting. Querying Gmail API...');
const query = encodeURIComponent('subject:"Verify your" to:' + echgEmail + ' newer_than:1h');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired — refresh at https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No matching verification email found in Gmail.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode verification email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found in verification email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Verification OTP stored in echg_vfy_code:', match[1]);
console.error('[ERROR] echg_vfy_code is empty — Gmail fetch in register step did not complete.');
console.error('        Check: gmail_access_token is set, token not expired, --delay-request >= 8000.');
console.log('[INFO] Access token stored in echg_access_token.');

item.name: happy-path
item.description:
Covers the complete three-step email-change wire path for the primary user.

New email target: +echgnew Gmail alias (same inbox, different to: address).

Order:
  step1 (request-change → 200) → fetch step1 OTP from Gmail →
  step2 (verify-current → 200) → fetch step2 OTP from Gmail →
  step3 (confirm-change → 200) →
  re-login with new email → GET /me confirm DB write.

After step3, echg_access_token is blocklisted. All subsequent auth uses echg_access_token_v2.

NOT covered here:
  - Token revocation side-effects (T-36, T-37): confirmed indirectly by re-login success.
  - DB-level KV state (T-11, T-23): covered by store_test.go.

item.name: POST /email/request-change → 200 (step 1 — send OTP to current email)

exec: // Derive the +echgnew Gmail alias from gmail_test_email.
// Gmail delivers user+echgnew@gmail.com to the same inbox as user@gmail.com.
// T-01 (step 1 wire path): JWT auth → rate limiter (echg:usr: slot 1) → handler → service → 200.
// --- Gmail step-1 OTP fetch (sent to current email address) ---
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for step-1 OTP email to arrive in Gmail...');
const query = encodeURIComponent('subject:"Your ' + appName + ' email change request" to:' + echgEmail + ' newer_than:1h');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired — refresh at https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No step-1 OTP email found in Gmail.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode step-1 email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found in step-1 email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Step-1 OTP stored in echg_step1_code:', match[1]);
console.error('[ERROR] echg_step1_code is empty — Gmail fetch in step-1 did not complete.');
// T-13 (step 2 wire path): JWT auth → rate limiter (echg:usr:vfy: slot 1) → handler → service → 200.
console.log('[INFO] Grant token stored in echg_grant_token.');
// --- Gmail step-2 OTP fetch (sent to new email address) ---
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for step-2 OTP email to arrive in Gmail...');
const query = encodeURIComponent('subject:"Confirm your new" to:' + echgNewEmail + ' newer_than:1h');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired — refresh at https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No step-2 OTP email found in Gmail for new email.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode step-2 email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found in step-2 email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Step-2 OTP stored in echg_step2_code:', match[1]);

item.name: POST /email/confirm-change → 200 (step 3 — update email, revoke all tokens)

exec: if (!gt) { console.error('[ERROR] echg_grant_token is empty — step-2 did not complete.'); }
if (!code) { console.error('[ERROR] echg_step2_code is empty — Gmail fetch in step-2 did not complete.'); }
// T-25 (step 3 wire path): JWT auth → rate limiter (echg:usr:cnf: slot 1) → handler → service → 200.
// After this call: users.email updated, all refresh tokens revoked, access token blocklisted.
// Store current new_email for use in failures folder same_email check.
// NOTE: echg_access_token is now blocklisted. Do not use it for further auth calls.
console.log('[INFO] Email change complete. echg_access_token is now invalid — re-login required.');

item.name: POST /login → 200 (re-login with new email — confirms new identifier active)

exec: // T-41 (indirect): successful login with the new email confirms the DB update.
// echg_access_token is blocklisted after step 3; this new token has full access.
console.log('[INFO] Re-login succeeded with new email. echg_access_token_v2 stored.');

item.name: GET /me → 200 (confirm users.email updated in DB — T-40)

exec: // T-40 — GET /me after step 3 confirms users.email column reflects the new address.
// This is distinct from T-25 which only checks the step-3 response shape.

item.name: failures
item.description:
Domain-level error cases. Uses a dedicated +echgfail user (fresh echg:usr: bucket). The primary user's echg:usr: bucket is down to 2 remaining slots after the happy-path step-1 call; using it here would exhaust the bucket before seed cooldown runs.

Slot accounting for echg_fail_access_token (burst=3, fresh bucket):
  echg:usr:   burst=3  — slots consumed: req1 (same_email), req2 (email_taken), req3 (seed cooldown) = 3 total.
  echg:usr:vfy: burst=5 — slots consumed: req4 (wrong code) = 1 total.
  echg:usr:cnf: burst=5 — slots consumed: req5 (invalid_grant_token) = 1 total.

NOT covered:
  - T-08 (ErrUserNotFound → 500): handler_test.go.
  - T-22 (KV pending absent → internal state): service_test.go.

exec: if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for +echgfail verification email...');
const query = encodeURIComponent('subject:"Verify your" to:' + failEmail + ' newer_than:1h');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No +echgfail verification email found.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode +echgfail email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No OTP found in +echgfail email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] +echgfail OTP stored in echg_fail_vfy_code:', match[1]);
if (!code) { console.error('[ERROR] echg_fail_vfy_code is empty — Gmail fetch in fail-setup 1/3 did not complete.'); }
console.log('[INFO] Fail access token stored in echg_fail_access_token.');
// Seed a user holding echg_taken@e2e.test so email_taken checks have a real conflict in DB.
// 409 is acceptable if a prior run left this user in the DB.
// T-05 — requesting change to the +echgfail user's own current email (echg_fail_email).
// Service ErrSameEmail guard fires → handler maps to 422 same_email.
// NOTE: consumes echg:usr: slot 1 for echg_fail user.
// T-06 — requesting change to an email registered to the seeded echg_taken user.
// NOTE: consumes echg:usr: slot 2 for echg_fail user.

item.name: POST /email/request-change → 200 (seed cooldown — 3rd and final echg:usr: slot)

exec: // Seed a pending verify token so that the next request triggers ErrCooldownActive.
// This is the 3rd and final slot in echg_fail user's echg:usr: bucket.
// NOTE: consumes echg:usr: slot 3 (last) for echg_fail user.
pm.test('status is 200 (OTP issued; cooldown window now active)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));
// T-07 — second request-change within 2-minute cooldown window → 429 cooldown_active.
// WHY BOTH ACCEPTED: echg:usr: burst=3 is now exhausted (3 slots consumed above).
// The 4th request hits echg:usr: rate limiter → 429 too_many_requests.
// The cooldown guard also fires, returning 429 cooldown_active.
// Which 429 fires first depends on guard ordering vs limiter order — both confirm wiring.
// The verify token seeded by the cooldown-seed request (req3) is still active for echg_fail user.
// Sending 000000 is virtually guaranteed to be wrong → ErrInvalidCode → 422 validation_error.
// NOTE: consumes echg:usr:vfy: slot 1 for echg_fail user.
// T-28 — grant_token not present in KV (nil UUID is not a real KV key).
// NOTE: consumes echg:usr:cnf: slot 1 for echg_fail user.

item.name: auth-failures
item.description:
Verifies that the JWTAuth middleware guards all three email-change endpoints.
No Authorization header → 401 on all three. JWT middleware fires before rate limiters — no slots consumed.

exec: // JWT middleware rejects missing header before rate limiter or handler body runs.
// NOTE: no echg:usr: slot consumed — JWT rejection fires before rate limiter.

item.name: validation
item.description:
Infrastructure-level request validation. Tests body > 1 MiB and malformed JSON on POST /email/request-change only — the same MaxBytesReader / DecodeJSON path is shared by all three handlers.

Uses echg_access_token_v2. echg:usr: bucket may be exhausted from failures folder (3 slots) — the 413 test therefore accepts 429 as well since rate limiter fires before MaxBytesReader.

exec: // Pad body past the 1 MiB MaxBytesReader limit.
// Rate limiter fires first — if bucket is exhausted this returns 429 instead of 413.
// Both are acceptable since both confirm the middleware pipeline is wired.
// Accept 413 (rate limiter passes, MaxBytesReader fires) or 429 (rate limiter exhausted).
// Rate limiter fires first, then decoder fails → 400 bad_request.
// If rate limiter is exhausted, returns 429 (also acceptable — RL wiring confirmed).

item.name: rate-limiting-req
item.description:
Verifies the echg:usr: per-user rate limiter for POST /email/request-change (burst=3, rate=3/10 min).

Why a dedicated user (+echgrlreq alias):
  echg_access_token_v2's echg:usr: bucket was exhausted by the failures + validation folders
  (3 slots consumed). A fresh user with a fresh bucket is required for a reliable burst=3 → 429 trigger.

Warmup notes:
  Slot 1: succeeds (→ 200, no cooldown yet).
  Slot 2: the same-target request-change fires within 2 min of slot 1 → 429 cooldown_active (from service).
    The test accepts 429 cooldown_active OR 200 (if targets differ) — it must NOT be 429 too_many_requests.
  Slot 3: same as slot 2.
  Slot 4: echg:usr: bucket exhausted → 429 too_many_requests (rate limiter fires before service guards).

Retry-After = ceil(1 / (3/600)) = ceil(200) = 200 seconds.

exec: // Pin _xff to 127.0.0.1 for all requests in this folder.
// echg:usr: keys on user ID from JWT, not IP. Pinning IP is a safety measure.
// Derive the +echgrlreq Gmail alias from gmail_test_email.
// Fetch the verification OTP for the +echgrlreq alias.
// to: filter ensures we target the alias email, not the main gmail_test_email.
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for +echgrlreq verification email...');
const query = encodeURIComponent('subject:"Verify your" to:' + rlEmail + ' newer_than:1h');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No +echgrlreq verification email found.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode +echgrlreq email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No OTP found in +echgrlreq email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] +echgrlreq OTP stored in echg_rl_req_vfy_code:', match[1]);
console.error('[ERROR] echg_rl_req_vfy_code is empty — Gmail fetch in rl-req-setup 1/3 did not complete.');
console.log('[INFO] RL-req access token stored in echg_rl_req_access_token.');

item.name: warmup 1/3 — POST /email/request-change → not 429 (echg:usr: slot 1 — first call succeeds)

exec: // Fresh bucket for +echgrlreq user. First request → 200 (OTP issued, no cooldown yet).
// Slot 2 of 3 consumed. This fires within 2 min of slot 1 → service returns 429 cooldown_active.
// The rate limiter has NOT exhausted (only 2 of 3 slots used) → must NOT be too_many_requests.
pm.test('not rate-limited by RL (echg:usr: slot 2 — cooldown_active is ok)', () => {
'slot 2 must produce cooldown_active (service guard), not too_many_requests (RL)');

item.name: warmup 3/3 — POST /email/request-change → not 429 too_many_requests (echg:usr: slot 3 — last)

exec: // Slot 3 of 3 consumed. Within cooldown window → service returns 429 cooldown_active.
// Rate limiter not yet exhausted (slot 3 is the last allowed) → must NOT be too_many_requests.
pm.test('not rate-limited by RL (echg:usr: slot 3 — last; cooldown_active is ok)', () => {
'slot 3 must produce cooldown_active (service guard), not too_many_requests (RL)');
// burst=3 exhausted. Rate limiter fires before service guards → 429 too_many_requests.
// NOT covered here: exact Retry-After value — covered by rate-limiter unit tests.

item.name: rate-limiting-vfy
item.description:
Verifies the echg:usr:vfy: per-user rate limiter for POST /email/verify-current (burst=5, rate=5/15 min).

Strategy: one step1 call seeds a verify token for the +echgrlvfy user; then five POST /email/verify-current calls with code '000000' each pass the rate limiter (slot consumed) and return 422 validation_error (wrong code). The 6th fires the RL and returns 429 too_many_requests.

Retry-After = ceil(1 / (5/900)) = ceil(180) = 180 seconds.

exec: if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for +echgrlvfy verification email...');
const query = encodeURIComponent('subject:"Verify your" to:' + rlEmail + ' newer_than:1h');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No +echgrlvfy verification email found.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode +echgrlvfy email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No OTP found in +echgrlvfy email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] +echgrlvfy OTP stored in echg_rl_vfy_vfy_code:', match[1]);
if (!code) { console.error('[ERROR] echg_rl_vfy_vfy_code is empty — Gmail fetch in rl-vfy-setup 1/3 did not complete.'); }
console.log('[INFO] RL-vfy access token stored in echg_rl_vfy_access_token.');

item.name: seed verify token — POST /email/request-change → 200 (active OTP now in DB)

exec: // Seeds a pending email_change_verify token for +echgrlvfy user.
// Warmup verify-current calls will find this token and return 422 (wrong code 000000).
// Code 000000 is virtually guaranteed wrong → rate limiter passes (slot 1) → 422 validation_error.
pm.test('not rate-limited yet (echg:usr:vfy: slot 5 — last allowed)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.eql(429));
// burst=5 exhausted. Rate limiter fires before handler → 429 too_many_requests.
// NOT covered here: exact Retry-After value — covered by rate-limiter unit tests.

item.name: rate-limiting-cnf
item.description:
Verifies the echg:usr:cnf: per-user rate limiter for POST /email/confirm-change (burst=5, rate=5/15 min).

Strategy: no valid grant_token needed. Five POST /email/confirm-change requests with a random nil-UUID grant_token each pass the rate limiter (slot consumed) and return 422 invalid_grant_token (KV lookup fails). The 6th fires the RL and returns 429 too_many_requests.

Retry-After = ceil(1 / (5/900)) = ceil(180) = 180 seconds.

exec: if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for +echgrlcnf verification email...');
const query = encodeURIComponent('subject:"Verify your" to:' + rlEmail + ' newer_than:1h');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No +echgrlcnf verification email found.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode +echgrlcnf email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No OTP found in +echgrlcnf email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] +echgrlcnf OTP stored in echg_rl_cnf_vfy_code:', match[1]);
if (!code) { console.error('[ERROR] echg_rl_cnf_vfy_code is empty — Gmail fetch in rl-cnf-setup 1/3 did not complete.'); }
console.log('[INFO] RL-cnf access token stored in echg_rl_cnf_access_token.');
// Nil-UUID grant_token → rate limiter passes (slot 1) → KV lookup fails → 422 invalid_grant_token.
pm.test('not rate-limited yet (echg:usr:cnf: slot 5 — last allowed)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.eql(429));
// burst=5 exhausted. Rate limiter fires before KV lookup → 429 too_many_requests.
// NOT covered here: exact Retry-After value — covered by rate-limiter unit tests.

variable.description: Auto-incremented counter used by the collection prerequest to generate unique X-Forwarded-For IPs.
Current X-Forwarded-For value. Set to a unique 10.x.x.x IP by the collection prerequest. Overridden to 127.0.0.1 by rate-limiting folder prerequest scripts.
Primary user email (from gmail_test_email). Set in setup/register prerequest.
Primary user password (P@ssw0rd1!). Set in setup/register prerequest.
Registration verification OTP for echg_email. Fetched from Gmail by setup/register test script.
Access token for primary user after initial login. Valid until step 3 (confirm-change) blocklists it.
Gmail +echgnew alias derived from gmail_test_email. Set in happy-path/step1 prerequest.
Step-1 OTP sent to echg_email (current address). Fetched from Gmail by happy-path/step1 test script.
Grant token returned by verify-current (step 2). Stored by happy-path/step2 test script. Used in step 3 body.
Step-2 OTP sent to echg_new_email (new address). Fetched from Gmail by happy-path/step2 test script.
Set to echg_new_email immediately after step 3 succeeds. Used by failures/same_email check.
Access token obtained by logging in with echg_new_email after the email change. Used by failures, auth-failures, and validation folders.
gmail_test_email+echgrlreq alias. Derived and set in rate-limiting-req/rl-req-setup 1/3 prerequest.
Password for echg_rl_req_email. Set in rate-limiting-req/rl-req-setup 1/3 prerequest.
Verification OTP for echg_rl_req_email. Fetched from Gmail by rl-req-setup 1/3 test script (to: filter targets +echgrlreq alias).
Access token for echg_rl_req_email. Fresh echg:usr: bucket — not used by any other folder.
gmail_test_email+echgrlvfy alias. Derived and set in rate-limiting-vfy/rl-vfy-setup 1/3 prerequest.
Password for echg_rl_vfy_email.
Verification OTP for echg_rl_vfy_email. Fetched from Gmail by rl-vfy-setup 1/3 test script.
Access token for echg_rl_vfy_email. Fresh echg:usr:vfy: bucket — used exclusively by rate-limiting-vfy.
gmail_test_email+echgrlcnf alias. Derived and set in rate-limiting-cnf/rl-cnf-setup 1/3 prerequest.
Password for echg_rl_cnf_email.
Verification OTP for echg_rl_cnf_email. Fetched from Gmail by rl-cnf-setup 1/3 test script.
Access token for echg_rl_cnf_email. Fresh echg:usr:cnf: bucket — used exclusively by rate-limiting-cnf.
gmail_test_email+echgfail alias. Derived and set in failures/fail-setup 1/3 prerequest. Used as the same_email (T-05) target (their own current address).
Password for echg_fail_email. Set in failures/fail-setup 1/3 prerequest.
Verification OTP for echg_fail_email. Fetched from Gmail by fail-setup 1/3 test script.
Access token for echg_fail_email. Fresh echg:usr: bucket (burst=3) — used exclusively by the failures folder.
